name: In the land of Lomagne garlic — Saint Clar loop
name_original: Au pays de l'ail de Lomagne — boucle de Saint Clar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Saint-Clar traverse varied landscapes in the Gers department of southwestern France. The region features mostly well-paved surfaces, making for smooth rides through agricultural vistas and historic elements. Cyclists can find routes catering to different fitness levels, with some offering significant elevation gain. The area provides a serene, rural environment for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Saint-Clar The most popular road cycling route is In the land of Lomagne garlic — Saint Clar loop, an 18.1 miles (29.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete, winding through the region's agricultural heartland. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Saint-Clar Village Center loop from Saint-Clar, a moderate 34.5 miles (55.6 km) path. This route offers a diverse ride starting from the village center, showcasing the local terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Bastide and Cologne hall – Medieval village of Sarrant loop from Saint-Clar, a 39.6 miles (63.7 km) trail leading through historic architecture and charming villages, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Road cycling around Saint-Clar is defined by agricultural vistas, historic architecture, and charming villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging excursions. The routes in Saint-Clar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Clar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Saint-Clar?
A: There are over 150 road cycling routes around Saint-Clar. You'll find a diverse selection, including 14 easy routes, 118 moderate routes, and 23 more challenging options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Saint-Clar?
A: The road cycling routes in Saint-Clar feature mostly well-paved surfaces, offering smooth rides. The terrain is varied, encompassing agricultural vistas, historic elements, and charming villages. While many routes are easy to ride, some include significant elevation gain, requiring good to very good fitness.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Saint-Clar?
A: Yes, Saint-Clar offers 14 easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: What are some longer or more challenging road cycling routes in the Saint-Clar area?
A: For those seeking a longer or more challenging ride, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26422795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide and Cologne hall – Medieval village of Sarrant loop from Saint-Clar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 63.7 km with significant elevation. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38744167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Clar Village Center loop from Saint-Clar&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 55.6 km path.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Saint-Clar?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Saint-Clar are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275700826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;In the land of Lomagne garlic — Saint Clar loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41134305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Clar Village Center – Château de Gramont loop from Saint-Léonard&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Saint-Clar?
A: Road cycling routes in Saint-Clar offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sights. You can pass through agricultural landscapes, charming villages, and historic architecture. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Clar Village Center&lt;/a&gt; itself, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Orens and Saint-Louis Church&lt;/a&gt;, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3201029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or villages to explore on a road bike near Saint-Clar?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5586378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Clar Village Center&lt;/a&gt;, known for its bastide architecture. Routes also lead to other historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plieux Village&lt;/a&gt;, and you might even encounter the impressive Cathedral of St. Gervasius and St. Protasius in Lectoure.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Saint-Clar?
A: The region's pleasant climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it may be warmer. Winters are typically mild, allowing for cycling, but always check local weather conditions.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Saint-Clar?
A: The road cycling experience in Saint-Clar is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the well-paved surfaces, and the serene rural environment that allows for engaging rides through agricultural vistas and historic villages.

Q: Are there family-friendly road biking options in Saint-Clar?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always available for road cycling, the 14 easy routes around Saint-Clar are generally suitable for families with older children or those looking for less strenuous rides. These routes offer a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take to complete in Saint-Clar?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in Saint-Clar varies significantly. Shorter loops, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275700826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;In the land of Lomagne garlic — Saint Clar loop&lt;/a&gt; (29.1 km), can take around 1 hour 30 minutes. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26422795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide and Cologne hall – Medieval village of Sarrant loop from Saint-Clar&lt;/a&gt; (63.7 km), might take closer to 2 hours 51 minutes, depending on your pace and stops.
tips:
The very pretty castle of Gramont belonged to Simon IV de Montfort, leader of the royal crusade against the Albigensians, who donated it to his crusade lieutenant, Odon de Montaut. The castle became a fortress in the 14th century with the construction of the Gothic entrance, then between 1535 and 1545 a Renaissance wing was built, placed at right angles to the old castle.
It was in the sixties that it was restored to regain its appearance.
Very pretty and old village which deserves the detour.

St Clar is a village in the Gers, build around a central church and main market place / square.  There are a number of cafes and restaurants here.

Just outside of town you will find the St Clar Animal Park, that houses Kangaroos.
